definition: Mineral value and derived public benefits reach countries irrespective of economic or scientific development.
Emerging space nations, universities, and public agencies can participate in missions, data, and contracts.
Mission knowledge, safety practices, and enabling infrastructure diffuse beyond first movers.
Rules are set through transparent multilateral governance, not only by mining states or firms.
Launch, reentry, orbital-debris, market, and terrestrial environmental risks are not shifted to low-power countries.
Resource extraction remains aligned with the Outer Space Treaty and does not militarize access to space resources.
